name: Python (Modern Best Practices)
description: Python 3.11+ with uv, Ruff, mypy strict, pytest, and src layout
conventions: Use uv for all package management — no pip install, no poetry, no conda
Ruff for linting and formatting — replaces black, flake8, isort, and pylint entirely
mypy in strict mode — all functions must have type annotations, no implicit Any
src layout: source code lives in src/<package_name>/ — tests in tests/ at root level
pytest for all tests — use fixtures for setup, parametrize for data-driven tests
Type hints on every function signature — use the latest syntax (list[str] not List[str])
Dataclasses or Pydantic for data models — never plain dicts for structured data
pathlib.Path for all file system operations — never os.path string manipulation
f-strings only for string formatting — never .format() or % formatting
No bare except: clauses — always catch a specific exception type
Context managers (with statements) for all resources — files, connections, locks
raise ValueError/TypeError with a descriptive message — never assert for runtime validation
Use __all__ in module __init__.py to define the public API explicitly
Log with the standard logging module — never print() in library code
Virtual environments managed by uv — .venv at project root, added to .gitignore
